title: Oldenlandia macraei
uses: Oldenlandia macraei is reported to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. It is said to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Some studies suggest that extracts from this plant may exhibit wound healing and antiseptic properties. Additionally, it is believed to have potential in treating fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Oldenlandia macraei for medicinal purposes.

intro: Oldenlandia macraei is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the madder family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, due to its reported health benefits of reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and acting as an antioxidant. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia macraei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
